destinations.evidence: Payload construction contains no SynchronousScheduler temporal-divergence eviction path before scheduled candidates execute.
destinations.snippet: Scheduled candidates are assembled from obligations/heartbeat/pool verification and then executed; no SynchronousScheduler::evict_call or scheduled execution block-header lookup appears.

destinations.evidence: Maintenance comments explicitly avoid resurrecting old scheduled chain-halt eviction; obligations are synthesized from parent state.
destinations.snippet: Raw scheduled txpool admission is dormant in this port; scheduled obligations are synthesized from parent state, so the old chain-halt scheduled-tx eviction loop is intentionally not resurrected here.

equivalence_argument: Mapped as absence of removed behavior: no temporal-divergence eviction, SynchronousScheduler dependency, or evict_call path.
rationale: The later removal states the payload-based mechanism is dead/nonfunctional for halts and handled elsewhere.
statement: Payload construction must not evict scheduled calls through the removed wall-clock temporal-divergence path that looked up scheduled execution block headers, called SynchronousScheduler::evict_call, and filtered scheduled_txs before execution.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build/search guard that ethereum/payload has no SynchronousScheduler or evict_call references.
summary: Temporal-divergence scheduled eviction is absent from payload construction.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Symbol search for SynchronousScheduler/evict_call in payload should remain empty.
title: Payload builder no longer performs temporal divergence eviction
